serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of flavoured oat milk is one glass of approximately 240 ml, providing around 127 kcal and 18.2 g of carbohydrates.

nutrient_source.title: Oat Milk, Flavoured — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F01020005 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this source without modification.

gi_source.title: Oat milk, glycemic index — University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 31 (low) is consistent with published University of Sydney GI Database values for oat-based beverages. Oat milk GI is reported in the range 30–36 in peer-reviewed literature (Wolever et al., 2003, Am J Clin Nutr). Glycemic load of 2.4 per 100 ml serving is calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g) / 100 = (31 × 7.6) / 100 = 2.36, rounded to 2.4. Flavoured variant assumed to have equivalent GI to unflavoured oat milk given sugar_g is reported as 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (11 mg/100 ml) and zero added sugar
hypertension.guidance: Flavoured oat milk contains only 11 mg sodium per 100 ml and no added sugar, making it compatible with DASH dietary principles; patients with hypertension may consume it freely within overall daily fluid and calorie targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (31), oat beta-glucan fibre, and zero added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 31, 0.75 g dietary fibre per 100 ml (including oat beta-glucan), and 0 g added sugar, flavoured oat milk has a low glycaemic impact and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard portions; portion control is advised as carbohydrates (7.6 g/100 ml) can accumulate with large servings.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.2 g/100 ml), oat beta-glucan, and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Flavoured oat milk provides 0.2 g saturated fat and 0 mg cholesterol per 100 ml, with oat-derived beta-glucan fibre that has demonstrated LDL-cholesterol-lowering effects per AHA guidelines; it is a heart-friendly dairy alternative suitable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Zero added sugar, low saturated fat, and calcium fortification
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Flavoured oat milk contains no added sugar, very low saturated fat (0.2 g/100 ml), and 65 mg calcium per 100 ml, supporting bone protection relevant for RA patients on corticosteroids; beta-glucan fibre provides modest anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured oat milk is a dairy-free, plant-based milk alternative produced by blending oats with water and adding natural flavourings, then straining to produce a smooth, mildly sweet-tasting beverage. It is fortified with calcium (65 mg/100 ml) and contains oat-derived beta-glucan dietary fibre (0.75 g/100 ml), which supports gut health and has clinically demonstrated LDL-cholesterol-lowering properties. Per 100 ml, it provides 53 kcal, 1.11 g protein, 2.22 g fat (of which approximately 0.2 g is saturated), and 7.6 g carbohydrates with 0 g added sugar. The sodium content is very low at 11 mg/100 ml. Its glycemic index is 31 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management. Flavoured oat milk is suitable for vegans, vegetarians, and those with lactose intolerance or dairy allergies, though it is not gluten-free due to oat content. It is commonly consumed at breakfast as a beverage or used as a dairy substitute in coffee, smoothies, and cereals. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease risk, provided portion sizes are controlled.
common_user_queries: Is oat milk good for diabetics?
Is flavoured oat milk dairy free?
How many calories in oat milk?
Is oat milk suitable for hypertension?
Does oat milk have added sugar?
Is oat milk good for heart health?
What is the GI of oat milk?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F01020005, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). GI value of 31 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimates for oat-based beverages and peer-reviewed literature. All core macros are non-zero and plausible.
notes: sugar_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB source for this specific flavoured oat milk record; this is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo (cr_id F01020005). Users should verify the product label as some commercial flavoured oat milks contain added sugars. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific flavoured oat milk are not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g of 0.2 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for oat milk (FDC ID 1097542) as the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat; this is documented as an adapted value. This food is not gluten-free due to oat content. GI glycemic load of 2.4 is calculated per 100 ml serving: (31 × 7.6) / 100 = 2.36, rounded to 2.4.
